name: Blood Pressure — Stage 1 Hypertension
message: Systolic BP ≥ 130 mmHg — Stage 1 hypertension. Lifestyle modification warranted.
actionRequired: Confirm on two separate occasions. Initiate lifestyle modifications: DASH diet, sodium restriction, exercise. Reassess in 3 months.
sourceAuthority: ACC/AHA 2017 Guideline for the Prevention, Detection, Evaluation, and Management of High Blood Pressure

name: Blood Pressure — Stage 2 Hypertension
message: Systolic BP ≥ 140 mmHg — Stage 2 hypertension. Pharmacological therapy indicated.
actionRequired: Initiate antihypertensive therapy per ACC/AHA protocol. Lifestyle modification. Recheck in 1 month. Evaluate for end-organ damage.
sourceAuthority: ACC/AHA 2017 Guideline for the Prevention, Detection, Evaluation, and Management of High Blood Pressure

name: Blood Pressure — Hypertensive Crisis
message: Systolic BP ≥ 180 mmHg — HYPERTENSIVE CRISIS. Immediate evaluation required.
actionRequired: URGENT: Assess for end-organ damage (hypertensive urgency vs emergency). Emergency department referral if symptoms present.
sourceAuthority: ACC/AHA 2017 Guideline for the Prevention, Detection, Evaluation, and Management of High Blood Pressure

name: SpO2 — Hypoxemia
message: SpO2 < 94% — hypoxemia. Supplemental oxygen may be required.
actionRequired: Assess for respiratory distress. Apply supplemental oxygen if symptomatic. Investigate underlying cause (pneumonia, CHF, PE). Escalate if SpO2 < 90%.
sourceAuthority: WHO 2021 Oxygen Therapy for Children (adapted for adults); BTS 2017 Emergency Oxygen Guidelines

name: SpO2 — Critical Hypoxemia
message: SpO2 < 90% — CRITICAL hypoxemia. Immediate intervention required.
actionRequired: URGENT: High-flow oxygen. Consider non-invasive ventilation. Emergency evaluation. Prepare for potential intubation.
sourceAuthority: BTS 2017 Emergency Oxygen Guidelines for Adults

name: Temperature — Fever
message: Temperature > 38.5°C — fever. Evaluate for infectious etiology.
actionRequired: Assess clinical context. Blood cultures if sepsis suspected. Antipyretics per protocol. Investigate source.

name: Heart Rate — Tachycardia
message: Heart rate > 100 bpm — tachycardia. Assess for underlying cause.
actionRequired: Evaluate for fever, dehydration, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, or cardiac pathology. ECG if sustained or symptomatic.
sourceAuthority: ACC/AHA 2015 Guideline for SVT, Section on Sinus Tachycardia
